Description: Verify the premium is calculated correctly when a rate with 6 decimal places is entered and the calculator uses full precision.
Steps: 1. Login to EZDocs. 2. Select LOB and product. 3. Create or open a quote. 4. Go to Total Insured Values -> Premium and click Add. 5. In the Premium modal enter Value ($) = 100000.00. 6. Set Rate Type = Rate (Number). 7. Enter Rate = 0.452345 (6 decimals). 8. Save modal. 9. Observe premium value in the Premium table and document preview.
Expected Results: - The system accepts the 6-decimal rate input. - Premium is calculated using the full 6-decimal rate precision (calculated premium matches the hand-calculated expected value). - No manual editing required to make totals match.

Description: Verify the premium is calculated correctly when a rate with 8 decimal places is entered.
Steps: 1. Login to EZDocs. 2. Select LOB and product. 3. Create or open a quote. 4. Open Premium modal. 5. Enter Value ($) = 250000.00. 6. Enter Rate = 0.45234567 (8 decimals). 7. Save and observe results. 8. Repeat with other values to confirm consistency.
Expected Results: - The system accepts the 8-decimal rate input. - Premium is calculated using the full 8-decimal precision and matches expected hand calculation. - Calculated totals match the Total Insured Values where applicable.

Description: Confirm the system rejects non-numeric input in the Rate field and shows validation message.
Steps: 1. Login and open a quote. 2. Open Premium modal. 3. Enter Value ($) = 50000.00. 4. Enter Rate = '0.45a234' (include letters). 5. Attempt to save.
Expected Results: - The system blocks save and shows a clear validation error near the Rate field (e.g., 'Please enter a valid numeric value'). - No invalid value is persisted.

Description: Ensure rates with 4 decimals still calculate correctly and are compatible with the new precision handling.
Steps: 1. Login and open a quote. 2. Open Premium modal. 3. Enter Value ($) = 120000.00. 4. Enter Rate = 0.4523 (4 decimals). 5. Save and verify premium.
Expected Results: - System accepts 4-decimal rate. - Premium calculation is correct and consistent with historical behavior for 4-decimal inputs. - No regression for 4-decimal cases.

Test Case Name: Print formatting shows up to 6 decimals (Positive)
Description: Verify document preview/print shows rate/premium formatted to 6 decimals while calculations use full precision.
Steps: 1. Login and create quote. 2. Add a premium line with Value = 100000 and Rate = 0.45234567 (8 decimals). 3. Save. 4. Generate preview document (English). 5. Inspect printed rate/premium formatting.
Expected Results: - Document preview/print displays rate and premium rounded/formatted to a maximum of 6 decimals. - The value used for calculation remains the higher-precision number; printed representation is limited to 6 decimals only.

Test Case Name: Premium matches Total Insured Values mapping (Integration/Positive)
Description: Ensure the premium calculator uses the same underlying insured values as the 'Total Insured Values' lines and maps correctly to premium lines.
Steps: 1. Login, select product, create quote. 2. Populate Total Insured Values list with one entry: Value ($) = 200000.00. 3. Go to Premium and add a premium line linked to this insured value. 4. Enter Rate = 0.00123456 (8 decimals). 5. Save and compare the premium calculation to the expected derived premium using the same insured value.
Expected Results: - Premium line calculation uses the exact Total Insured Value entry. - Calculated premium is consistent with hand calculation using the same insured value and rate precision. - No mismatch between the Total Insured Values and Premium lines.

Test Case Name: Manual override no longer required when calculator works (Regression/Positive)
Description: Confirm that when the calculator produces the correct premium, manual entry of premium is not needed and the UI does not force manual edits.
Steps: 1. Login and open a quote. 2. Create Total Insured Value and a Premium line. 3. Enter a high-precision rate (e.g., 0.45234567). 4. Observe whether premium is auto-populated. 5. Try to save without manual premium edits.
Expected Results: - Premium auto-populates correctly. - Save succeeds without needing manual premium entry. - System does not prompt user to manually enter premium to match totals.

Description: Validate behavior when rate is zero or extremely small/high precision values (0, 0.00000001) and ensure calculations are stable.
Steps: 1. Login and open quote. 2. Add Premium line with Value = 100000.00. 3. Enter Rate = 0.00000000 and save — observe. 4. Repeat with Rate = 0.00000001 (8 decimals). 5. Repeat with Rate = 1.00000000 (1.0).
Expected Results: - Rate = 0 results in premium = 0 with no calculation error. - Very small rates compute premium accurately (no underflow or incorrect rounding to zero unless mathematically zero). - Very large/1.0 rates compute correctly and UI remains stable.

Description: Verify rate and premium values persist across quote save, document preview, binder creation and reopen.
Steps: 1. Login and create quote. 2. Add premium line with Value = 150000 and Rate = 0.45234567. 3. Save quote. 4. Preview document. 5. Create binder or simulate save-to-binder. 6. Reopen quote and inspect premium and rate values.
Expected Results: - Rate and premium persist across save and lifecycle events. - Reopened quote shows same stored rate (full precision) and premium (calculated). - Preview and binder show consistent values (with print formatting as defined).

Description: Confirm the system rejects malformed numeric formats (multiple decimal points, leading/trailing symbols) and shows errors.
Steps: 1. Login and open premium modal. 2. Enter Value = 100000. 3. Enter Rate = '0..4523' or '%.4523' or '--0.4523'. 4. Attempt to save.
Expected Results: - Save blocked; clear validation message shown. - Input is not persisted until corrected. - Error messages are user-friendly and positioned near the field.

Test Case Name: Empty required Rate field prevents save (Negative)
Description: Ensure required validation prevents save when a mandatory Rate is left blank for calculator-required lines.
Steps: 1. Login and create quote. 2. Add premium line that requires a rate for calculation. 3. Leave Rate blank. 4. Attempt to save the premium modal.
Expected Results: - System blocks save and indicates the Rate field is required with an inline validation message. - No null or default incorrect premium is created.

Test Case Name: Stored calculation precision vs printed precision (Positive)
Description: Verify the system stores and uses high-precision rate for calculations but prints/exports a rounded representation (6 decimals) only.
Steps: 1. Login and add premium line with Value = 123456.78 and Rate = 0.452345678 (9 decimals). 2. Save and inspect stored (backend/API) value if available or observe UI summary showing exact stored value. 3. Generate preview and inspect printed values.
Expected Results: - Backend/storage retains the high-precision rate used for calculations. - Calculated premium matches high-precision computation. - Document preview/print shows values rounded/formatted to 6 decimals for readability.
